title: Flaky Aerated Middle Eastern Fish Ouzi
description: A rich and adventurous Middle Eastern dish featuring salt-baked fish, infused with the tang of white vinegar and the comfort of aromatic spices, all wrapped in flaky pastry. This pescatarian-friendly ouzi is a true delight for the senses.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the cod fish with salt, black pepper, cumin, and coriander.
In a large bowl, whisk together white vinegar, olive oil, and minced garlic.
Add the chopped onion to the bowl and mix well.
Place the seasoned cod fish in the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the flaky pastry on a lightly floured surface to a large rectangle.
Remove the fish from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Place the fish in the center of the pastry rectangle, leaving a 1-inch border around it.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water, then fold the pastry over the fish, pressing gently to seal.
Place the ouzi on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the ouzi from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
